title: haplopappus bezanillanus
intro: Haplopappus bezanillanus is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, known for its small, yellow daisy-like flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to aid in wound healing and reduce pain. The health benefits of Haplopappus bezanillanus include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and act as an antioxidant, protecting against cell damage and oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used externally to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
